name: Sony WF-1000XM6 Wireless Earbuds
verdict: The best wireless earbuds overall — unmatched sound quality, elite noise cancellation, and Sony's legendary audio tuning.
description: The Sony WF-1000XM6 are the earbuds audiophiles recommend. The new Dynamic Driver X delivers deep, textured bass alongside crystal-clear mids and highs. Noise cancellation rivals over-ear headphones thanks to the V2 processor, and the memory foam tips create a seal that blocks ambient noise before ANC even kicks in. Battery life hits 8 hours with ANC on (24 total with case). The companion app offers a graphic EQ, LDAC high-res streaming, and a 'Background Music' ambiance feature. At $298, they're premium-priced but deliver a listening experience nothing else in this form factor can match.
features: Dynamic Driver X for audiophile-grade sound
Memory foam tips for superior passive isolation
pros: Best sound quality in any wireless earbud
Noise cancellation rivals over-ear headphones
Memory foam tips fit most ears comfortably
LDAC support for high-res streaming
cons: Premium price — nearly $300
Foam tips degrade and need replacing
No multipoint Bluetooth connection
Touch controls can be finicky

name: Apple AirPods Pro 3
verdict: The best earbuds for iPhone users — hearing aid mode, heart rate sensing, and the deepest Apple ecosystem integration.
description: The AirPods Pro 3 aren't just earbuds — they're health devices. Built-in heart rate sensors track your pulse during workouts, and the FDA-cleared Hearing Aid mode can amplify ambient sound for users with mild hearing loss. Noise cancellation has been significantly improved over the Pro 2, and Adaptive Audio automatically blends ANC and transparency based on your environment. Five ear tip sizes ensure a good fit, and spatial audio with head tracking creates an immersive listening experience. If you own an iPhone, these are the earbuds to get — nothing else integrates as deeply with iOS.
features: Built-in heart rate sensor for workouts
Adaptive Audio blends ANC + transparency automatically
5 ear tip sizes for better fit
Spatial audio with dynamic head tracking
pros: Deepest iPhone/Apple ecosystem integration
Heart rate + hearing aid features are unique
5 ear tip sizes fit more ears than competitors
Adaptive Audio is genuinely useful
cons: Full features require iPhone — limited on Android
No LDAC or high-res codec support
$249 is steep for non-audiophile sound
Case scratches easily

name: Bose QuietComfort Ultra Earbuds (2nd Gen)
verdict: The best noise-canceling earbuds — Bose's ANC is still the gold standard for silencing your surroundings.
description: When pure noise cancellation is the priority, Bose still wears the crown. The QC Ultra Earbuds Gen 2 block more ambient noise than any other earbud tested — subway rumble, airplane engines, open-office chatter all virtually disappear. Sound quality has improved significantly with Bose Immersive Audio, adding spatial depth to music and podcasts. The new wireless charging case is a welcome addition, and 6 hours of battery with ANC on (24 total) gets through a full workday. Aware mode with ActiveSense lets through voices while still blocking background noise — perfect for office use.
features: Bose Immersive Audio for spatial sound
ActiveSense aware mode filters voices vs noise
CustomTune auto-calibrates to your ear shape
pros: Best noise cancellation available in earbuds
Immersive Audio adds real spatial depth
ActiveSense is the best transparency mode tested
Wireless charging case is a welcome upgrade
cons: Sound quality still trails Sony for music purists
$249 for earbuds is premium
Ear tips are proprietary — no third-party options
Bulkier than competitors

name: Samsung Galaxy Buds 4 Pro
verdict: The best earbuds for Samsung users — seamless Galaxy integration, 360 Audio, and excellent call quality.
description: If you live in the Samsung ecosystem, the Galaxy Buds 4 Pro are the obvious choice. They auto-switch between your Galaxy phone, tablet, and laptop, and the Galaxy Wearable app offers deep customization including a 9-band EQ, 360 Audio head tracking, and ANC level adjustments. Call quality is exceptional — the 3-mic system with AI noise reduction makes you sound clear even in noisy environments. ANC performance has caught up to Bose and Sony, and the blade-style design sits flush in your ear. SSC Hi-Fi codec delivers studio-quality streaming from Samsung devices.
features: SSC Hi-Fi codec for Samsung devices
pros: Best integration with Samsung devices
Call quality is best-in-class
ANC has caught up to competitors
Blade design is comfortable for all-day wear
cons: Full features require Samsung Galaxy device
SSC codec only works with Samsung
$250 is premium pricing
Touch controls have a learning curve

name: EarFun Air Pro 4+
verdict: The best budget ANC earbuds — 80% of the flagship experience at 30% of the price.
description: The EarFun Air Pro 4+ prove you don't need to spend $250 for excellent noise-canceling earbuds. At $80 (often on sale for less), they deliver ANC that genuinely silences commuter noise, sound quality that punches well above their price with a warm, detailed signature, and 11 hours of battery per charge. LDAC support means you can stream high-res audio from Android devices. The app offers a customizable EQ, ANC level adjustment, and game mode with reduced latency. PCMag gave them an Editors' Choice. If you're not an audiophile, these might be all the earbuds you ever need.
features: Active noise cancellation at a budget price
11hr battery per charge (52hr total with case)
Customizable EQ and ANC in companion app
pros: Incredible value — ANC earbuds for $80
LDAC support is rare at this price
11-hour battery life is outstanding
PCMag Editors' Choice winner
cons: Build quality doesn't match $250 competitors
ANC isn't as strong as Sony or Bose
Microphone quality is average for calls
No wireless charging on the case

name: Google Pixel Buds Pro 2
verdict: The best earbuds for Pixel and Android users — Gemini AI integration, superb fit, and Google's clean software.
description: Google's Pixel Buds Pro 2 are the most comfortable earbuds in this roundup — their small, rounded design with stabilizer fins stays put during workouts without creating ear fatigue during long listening sessions. The headline feature is Gemini AI integration — ask questions, get translations, and control your phone hands-free with natural conversation. ANC is solid (not Bose-level, but effective), and the sound signature is balanced and natural. Google's Find My Device integration means you'll never lose them. At $229, they're for Android users who want the cleanest, most integrated experience.
features: Tensor A1 chip for on-device processing
pros: Most comfortable earbuds tested — all-day wearable
Gemini AI integration is genuinely useful
Conversation Detection works flawlessly
Excellent Google/Android integration
cons: ANC trails Sony and Bose
Sound quality is good but not audiophile-grade
$229 for mid-tier ANC is steep
Limited features on iOS

name: Anker Soundcore Liberty 4 NC
verdict: The cheapest good ANC earbuds — under $60 with adaptive noise cancellation and 10-hour battery.
description: At $57, the Soundcore Liberty 4 NC are the most affordable noise-canceling earbuds worth recommending. Adaptive ANC adjusts to your environment in real-time, and the 98.5% noise reduction claim holds up well against commuter noise and office chatter. Sound quality is warm and bass-forward — great for pop and hip-hop, less ideal for classical. 10 hours of battery per charge is excellent, and the case adds another 40 hours. The Soundcore app offers a full EQ, ANC customization, and even a HearID test that personalizes sound to your hearing profile. NBC Select's 'Best Budget' pick.
pros: Cheapest ANC earbuds worth buying
10-hour battery is outstanding for the price
HearID personalization is a nice touch
Adaptive ANC works surprisingly well
cons: Bass-heavy sound signature won't suit everyone
Build quality is plastic and budget-feeling
Call quality is below average
No LDAC or high-res codec

name: Beats Fit Pro 2
verdict: The best earbuds for workouts — secure wingtip design, IPX4 sweat resistance, and works great with both iPhone and Android.
description: Beats Fit Pro 2 solve the biggest problem with workout earbuds: they stay put. The flexible wingtip design locks into your ear's concha ridge, and they won't budge during running, jumping, or weight training. Powered by Apple's H2 chip, they get ANC, transparency mode, and spatial audio on both iOS and Android (most Apple chip earbuds limit Android features). Sound is punchy and bass-forward — perfect for workout playlists. 6 hours of battery with ANC on, and a 5-minute quick charge gives an hour of playback. If fitness is your primary use case, these are the ones.
features: Apple H2 chip — works with iPhone AND Android
Spatial audio with dynamic head tracking
pros: Most secure fit for workouts — never fall out
Full features on both iOS and Android
Punchy, bass-forward sound for exercise
Quick charge is genuinely useful
cons: Sound is bass-heavy — not for audiophiles
Wingtips may not fit all ear shapes
No wireless charging case
$200 is mid-premium pricing

name: Shokz OpenRun Pro 2 Bone Conduction Headphones
verdict: The best open-ear earbuds — hear your surroundings while running, cycling, or walking with bone conduction audio.
description: The Shokz OpenRun Pro 2 don't go in your ears at all — they rest on your cheekbones and transmit sound through vibration. This means your ear canals stay completely open, so you hear traffic, conversations, and your environment while listening to music or podcasts. For runners, cyclists, and outdoor exercisers, this is a safety feature no in-ear bud can match. Sound quality has improved dramatically over previous Shokz models — bass is surprisingly present and mids are clear. 12 hours of battery, IP55 water resistance, and a titanium frame that flexes without breaking make these built for serious athletes.
features: Bone conduction — ears stay completely open
pros: Safest option for outdoor exercise — full awareness
12-hour battery outlasts all in-ear competitors
Titanium frame is virtually indestructible
Sound quality has improved dramatically
cons: Sound quality can't match in-ear earbuds
Bass is limited by bone conduction physics
No noise cancellation (by design)
Vibration can feel odd at high volumes

name: Status Pro X Wireless Earbuds
verdict: Forbes' #1 pick — the best balance of sound, ANC, and battery life at a mid-range price.
description: The Status Pro X came out of nowhere to claim Forbes' top pick for 2026. The 12mm drivers deliver remarkably detailed sound with a balanced signature that works across all genres. ANC is effective without the pressure feeling some competitors create. The standout is battery life: 10 hours per charge with ANC on, and 40 hours total with the case. Physical buttons (not touch controls) mean no accidental taps, and the IP55 rating handles workouts and rain. At $130, they undercut the big three (Sony, Bose, Apple) by $120+ while delivering 90% of the experience.
pros: Forbes #1 pick for 2026
Best battery life with ANC in this price range
Physical buttons are a welcome change
Sound quality punches above $130
cons: Relatively unknown brand
No LDAC or high-res codec
App is basic compared to Sony/Bose
No wireless charging
